@eskeFussballWed 19:43June 24, 2026 at 19:43
World Cup: Hoffenheim trio start for Czechia against co-hosts Mexico
Czechia eye to join co-hosts Mexico in the Round of 32 at the World Cup.
Lars Baron/Getty Images
Mexico host Czechia in their last group match, having already secured their place in the Round of 32 as Group A winners. Czechia took the lead against South Korea and South Africa, but they have only taken a single point so far in the competition.
Hoffenheim trio – Robin Hranáč, Vladimír Coufal, and Adam Hložek – are in Czechia’s starting lineup once again, while Patrik Schick is relegated to the substitute bench after going scoreless in the first two games.
17-year-old Mexican midfielder Gilberto Mora starts for the first time in the World Cup, while 40-year-old iconic goalkeeper Guillermo Ochoa remains on the bench as he hopes to feature in his fourth tournament.
México XI: Rangel; Sánchez, Reyes, Montes, Chávez; Mora, Romo, Álvarez ©; Alvarado, Martínez, Quiñones
Czechia XI: Kovář; Hranáč, Holeš, Krejčí ©; Doudera, Červ, Sadilek, Coufal; Višinský, Šulc; Hložek
